rationale_top3: Standout waterpark entertainment with multiple pools, heated options, and splash park that families describe as 'like a mini Wet 'N' Wild', keeping kids entertained for hours with additional basketball courts, tennis courts, and games room Consistently clean amenities and spacious ensuite sites with reviews praising 'spotless amenities', 'super clean ensuite', and 'very clean camp kitchen', delivering reliable cleanliness standards across most facilities Excellent urban convenience as a city stopover with walking distance to Stockland Mall, supermarkets, and automotive services, supported by friendly staff who 'went above and beyond' and strong repeat visitation rates
rationale_honourable: Discovery Parks Rockhampton excels as a family destination with its standout waterpark and splash facilities that keep kids entertained for hours. The park offers excellent ensuite sites, consistently clean amenities, and friendly staff who genuinely care about guest experience. While the urban location and occasional maintenance issues hold it back from Gold status, families with young children will find tremendous value in the on-site entertainment and well-maintained facilities that make this a reliable stopover or destination stay.
water_fun: Fantastic waterpark and splash park for kids
kids_play: Waterpark, pools, basketball, tennis, games room available
pet_detail: Pet friendly - dogs welcome at park
best_for: Families with children under 12 who prioritize water play entertainment and convenient access to city amenities over natural bushland settings
executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Rockhampton
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 216

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Reviews consistently praise the waterpark as the star attraction with families saying "water park was a hit", "kids loved the waterpark", and describing it as "like a mini Wet 'N' Wild". Multiple pools including heated options, splash park, basketball courts, tennis courts, and games room provide extensive entertainment. Some reviewers noted the waterpark "could use some love" and equipment showing age, preventing a perfect score.

Nature (12/20) — The park is in an urban/industrial location with reviews noting "en pleine zone industrielle" and being "on the Bruce Highway". Positive mentions include "bird life was amazing" and "quiet enough to hear the birds chirp". However, this is clearly a city park with proximity to shopping centres and service stations rather than natural bushland, beaches, or creek access.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Strong consensus on cleanliness with "spotless amenities", "super clean ensuite", "very clean amenities", and "camp kitchen very clean and tidy" appearing frequently. However, multiple concerning reviews mention "mould in bathroom drain", "black mould all over the ceiling", "mould in shower", and "old and shabby" cabins, indicating inconsistency across accommodation types.

Value (13/15) — Generally positive value perception with "price was very good", "$32 a night powered sites good value", "super affordable", and "reasonable pricing". However, several reviews highlight overpricing: "$380 a night" for run-down cabin described as "extremely poor", "$260 a night" without working air conditioning, and "$51 powered site" difficulties, showing significant price variations.

Site Size (8/10) — Positive feedback includes "spacious site", "good size sites", "generous sized van sites", "huge site", and "tons of room for our 29 foot motorhome". Negatives include "sites very tight for van", "hard to get our van on the two strips of concrete", "slightly crowded sites", and some caravans finding it "very unlevel, on a hill".

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong repeat visitation with "stayed numerous times", "second stay", "will definitely be back", and "favourite place to stop". Staff praised as "wonderful", "friendly", "went above and beyond". However, serious complaints about customer service ("refused to refund", "management never called back"), theft incidents ("2 bikes stolen", "lock up your bikes"), and poor complaint handling lower the perfect sentiment score.

Location (5/10) — Mixed location feedback: praised for convenience with "close to everything", "walking distance from supermarkets", "20 minute walk to Stockland Mall", "Repco and Battery World across the road". However, urban setting means "heavy traffic", "street noise", "adjacent to a school", and "nothing nearby" for those seeking nature. Not a destination location but excellent for city access.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Serious and recurring maintenance issues with mould in multiple cabins and bathrooms, broken air conditioning units, and aging facilities that don't match promotional photos, indicating inconsistent property standards across accommodation types.
2. Urban industrial location on the Bruce Highway lacks natural environment, beach access, or bushland setting that many families seek, making it purely a convenient city stopover rather than a nature-based holiday destination.
3. Security and operational concerns including multiple theft incidents, local day visitors overcrowding facilities that paying guests booked for, inconsistent customer service, and management's poor handling of legitimate complaints about health hazards.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"water park was a hit", "kids loved the waterpark", "super clean ensuite", "perfect for families", "mould all over the ceiling", "locals use all the facilities", "staff went above and beyond", "tired park needs some love", "fantastic waterpark", "would definitely stay again", "manager refused to refund", "2 bikes stolen lock everything up"

rationale_top3: Complete absence of kids entertainment or activities with a neglected games room that families found unusable, making this unsuitable as a destination for families with young children seeking engagement. Severely compromised site sizes that prevent awning deployment and create cramped conditions, with families reporting feeling "squashed" by neighboring vans and inadequate space for typical family camping setups. Deeply inconsistent cleanliness and maintenance standards in aging facilities, with multiple families reporting filthy bathrooms, broken fixtures, deteriorating cabins with rot, and an overall impression that "owners have lost interest" in upkeep.
rationale_honourable: Riverside Tourist Park offers a budget-friendly riverside location within walking distance to Rockhampton's CBD, making it convenient for families seeking an affordable overnight or short-term stopover. While facilities show significant age and maintenance issues, the park provides basic amenities including a pool, clean showers with good water pressure, and shaded sites under mature trees along the Fitzroy River, appealing to families who prioritize location and value over modern facilities.
water_fun: Basic pool available, noted as clean
kids_play: Games room outdated, no activities mentioned
pet_detail: Pet friendly noted in several reviews
best_for: Families seeking the most affordable accommodation option in Rockhampton for a quick overnight stop who can overlook dated facilities in exchange for excellent value and central location.
executive_summary:
PARK: Riverside Tourist Park
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 110

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews consistently report no organized activities or kids entertainment, with the games room described as "creepy" with "magazines from 2017" and "smells like something not cleaned in a long time." Families noted "no activities planned for people staying" and the overall entertainment offering is essentially non-existent for young children.

Nature (13/20) — The park sits directly on the Fitzroy River with "plenty of trees" providing shade and a "tropical feeling garden" atmosphere. Families appreciated the riverside location for sunsets and the natural bush setting, though this comes with significant mosquito and sandfly infestations that multiple reviews warned about. River access for fishing was noted as limited.

Cleanliness (7/15) — Reviews are deeply divided: some found amenities "always clean and tidy" with "lovely hot showers," while many others reported "terrible amenities block. So outdated and filthy," "bathrooms were filthy," and "camp kitchen had no plugs for washing up and was dirty and uncared for." The consensus suggests inconsistent cleaning standards in aging facilities.

Value (10/15) — At $38-42 per night, families acknowledged "great value for money" and "cheap accommodation" for the central location. However, multiple reviews questioned value, stating "for $41/night, no value for money" and "It's cheap - for a reason," with cabin stays deemed overpriced at $160 for the condition provided.

Site Size (4/10) — This was a major complaint across reviews: "tight campsites right on top of each other," "super tight space for the van. Could not put awning out," "sites are small," and "some sites extremely narrow and tight." Families with larger rigs consistently struggled with inadequate space.

Sentiment (6/10) — Mixed family sentiment with some repeat visitors appreciating the no-frills approach, but many families stated they "wouldn't go again," "would not visit again," and "won't be back." Service issues were common with reports of unprofessional staff, unanswered phones, and inflexible policies that frustrated families.

Location (8/10) — The park's strongest asset is its proximity to Rockhampton CBD with "walking distance to city centre," "15 minute walk to pubs and restaurants," and "close to all shopping centres." However, this urban location brings significant traffic noise from the nearby Fitzroy bridge that disrupted sleep for many families.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of kids entertainment or activities with a neglected games room that families found unusable, making this unsuitable as a destination for families with young children seeking engagement.
2. Severely compromised site sizes that prevent awning deployment and create cramped conditions, with families reporting feeling "squashed" by neighboring vans and inadequate space for typical family camping setups.
3. Deeply inconsistent cleanliness and maintenance standards in aging facilities, with multiple families reporting filthy bathrooms, broken fixtures, deteriorating cabins with rot, and an overall impression that "owners have lost interest" in upkeep.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Terrible amenities block. So outdated and filthy!", "tight campsites right on top of each other", "infested with sandflies", "No activities planned for people staying", "games room was creepy", "owners have lost interest", "Walking distance to city centre", "Cheap accommodation great value"
